simple_types.name: DCRSR_HALTED_Field
simple_types.code:
type DCRSR_HALTED_Field is
  (
   Register_0,
   Register_1,
   Register_2,
   Register_3,
   Register_4,
   Register_5,
   Register_6,
   Register_7,
   …,
   Control_Faultmask_Basepri_Primask)
  with Size => 5;

simple_types.name: DCRSR_REGWnR_Field
simple_types.code:
type DCRSR_REGWnR_Field is
  (
   Read,
   Write)
  with Size => 1;

record_types.name: DCRSR_Register
Debug_Peripheral

fields.name: DFSR
fields.description: Debug Halting Control and Status Register

fields.name: DHCSR
fields.description:
Debug Core Register Selector Register: The DCRSR write-only register
generates a handshake to the core to transfer the selected register
to/from the DCRDR. The DHCSR S_REGRDY bit is cleared when the DCRSR
is written, and remains clear until the core transaction completes.
This register is only accessible from Debug state.

fields.name: DCRDR
fields.description: Debug Exception and Monitor Control Register

record_types.name: DEMCR_Register
DFSR_Register

fields.name: HALTED
fields.description: BKPT instruction executed or breakpoint match in FPB.

fields.name: BKPT
fields.description:
Data Watchpoint and Trace trap. Indicates that the core halted due to
at least one DWT trap event.

fields.name: DWTTRAP
fields.description:
Vector catch triggered. Corresponding FSR will contain the primary
cause of the exception.

fields.name: VCATCH
fields.description: An asynchronous exception generated due to the assertion of EDBGRQ.

record_types.name: DHCSR_Cluster
Read_DHCSR_Register
Write_DHCSR_Register

fields.name: Reserved_6_15
fields.description:
Write-only. Debug Key. The value 0xA05F must be written to enable
write accesses to bits [15:0], otherwise the write access will be
ignored. Read behavior of bits [31:16] is as listed below.

variables.name: Debug_Periph
variables.code:
Debug_Periph : aliased Debug_Peripheral
  with Import, Address => Debug_Base;
